purpose:
Generic two-button confirmation dialog. Receives a title, message, onConfirm, and onCancel callback. Backed by Modal base — onClose maps to onCancel so ESC key and backdrop click also cancel. Renders

+ a row of buttons (.modal-btn-cancel / .modal-btn-confirm). Used app-wide whenever destructive or branching user intent must be captured outside of native confirm().
gotchas: ESC and backdrop-click both fire onCancel because onClose is wired to onCancel — there is no way to wire ESC to onConfirm or to disable cancel via dismissal.

Message is a single string rendered in a

— passing JSX or multi-paragraph content will render inside one paragraph and may need styling tweaks.
There is no busy/loading state — once Confirm is clicked, the modal closes immediately (handlers are responsibility of the parent). For async confirms, parent must show its own progress indicator.
patterns: Buttons follow naming convention .modal-btn + variant suffix (.modal-btn-cancel, .modal-btn-confirm, .modal-btn-close).
Companion to ErrorModal — both are minimal wrappers over Modal with stylized actions.
conventions: Default export is the component named like the file.
